title: artemisia maritima var. suffruticosa
intro: Artemisia maritima var. suffruticosa, also known as maritime wormwood, is a variety of the artemisia maritima plant, a perennial shrub-like herb native to coastal areas in Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which are derived from its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of maritime wormwood include relief from digestive issues, colds, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of arthritis. Maritime wormwood can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies and folk medicine to support overall health and well-being.
